personas.name: Anxious Student Seeking Immediate Emotional Support
personas.description: A university student experiencing anxiety, stress, or emotional overload who uses Wysa for immediate coping support in a private and judgment-free setting. This user values fast access to calming tools and emotionally appropriate responses during difficult moments.
pain_points: Feeling overwhelmed and needing support urgently
Responses may feel repetitive or too generic
context: Using the app during panic, stress, or emotional overload
Opening the app between classes or late at night
constraints: The interaction must feel private and emotionally safe
personas.notes: Refined from the automated persona by making the user context more concrete and focusing on urgency of support.

personas.name: Sleep-Deprived User Looking for Low-Effort Relaxation
personas.description: A user who struggles to relax or fall asleep and turns to Wysa at bedtime for calming exercises, guided support, and low-effort sleep content. This user wants soothing features that are easy to access with minimal interaction.
goals: Access calming content with minimal effort
pain_points: Difficulty finding the right sleep feature quickly
Some useful sleep content may be limited or locked
context: Using the app in bed before sleeping
Seeking help during nighttime stress or restlessness
constraints: Sleep features must be easy to reach
Interaction should be minimal and calming
personas.notes: Refined from the automated persona by emphasizing bedtime context and minimal-interaction expectations.

personas.name: Reflective User Practicing Journaling and CBT
personas.description: A user who uses Wysa regularly for journaling, guided self-reflection, and CBT-based exercises. They are motivated by self-improvement and want structured tools that help them better understand and reframe their thoughts.
pain_points: Difficulty translating feelings into clear written reflections
context: Using the app during quiet personal time
Returning to journaling or CBT activities regularly
constraints: Exercises must feel relevant and structured
personas.notes: Refined from the automated persona by clarifying that this user is motivated by recurring self-reflection rather than one-time crisis support.

personas.name: Cost-Conscious User Evaluating Whether Premium Is Worth It
personas.description: A user who sees value in Wysa but is frustrated by subscription restrictions and wants clear information about what is free, what is premium, and whether paying is justified. This user is sensitive to both cost and transparency.
goals: Understand what features are free versus paid
Access enough value before committing to premium
Judge whether the subscription is worth the price
pain_points: Useful features are hidden behind paywalls
Subscription value is not always obvious
Free experience may feel too limited
context: Exploring the app before deciding to subscribe
Using free features while comparing premium value
constraints: Premium options must be explained clearly
personas.notes: Refined from the automated persona by focusing more explicitly on transparency and purchase decision support.

personas.name: User Whose Trust Depends on Technical Reliability
personas.description: A user who wants to rely on Wysa for mental health support but loses trust when the app crashes, freezes, fails to load, or causes login issues. Because the app is used during important moments, reliability is especially critical for this user.
goals: Use the app reliably without technical interruptions
Log in successfully and access support quickly
Trust the app during important moments
pain_points: Login problems block access to support
Unstable performance reduces confidence in the app
context: Using the app when support is urgently needed
Returning to saved or familiar features across sessions
constraints: Login and loading must work reliably
personas.notes: Refined from the automated persona by emphasizing trust and the higher stakes of technical failure in a mental health app.
